# pi-listen

> Hold-to-talk voice input for the Pi coding agent CLI.

## Overview

pi-listen adds streaming voice-to-text input to Pi. Hold SPACE to record, release to transcribe — text appears in the editor in real time via Deepgram Nova 3 WebSocket streaming. Pre-records during warmup so you never miss the first word. Tail-records 1.5s after release so your last word isn't clipped.

## Setup

1. `pi install npm:@codexstar/pi-listen` (outside Pi)
2. Get key at https://dpgr.am/pi-voice ($200 free credit)
3. `export DEEPGRAM_API_KEY="your-key"` in ~/.zshrc
4. Open Pi — auto-activates, or run `/voice-setup`

Audio capture auto-detects: sox (preferred) → ffmpeg → arecord (Linux). No mandatory install.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/codexstar69/pi-listen/blob/main/README.md): Setup, usage, features
- [Troubleshooting](https://github.com/codexstar69/pi-listen/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting.md): Common issues
- [Changelog](https://github.com/codexstar69/pi-listen/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release history
- [License](https://github.com/codexstar69/pi-listen/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT

## Components

- `extensions/voice.ts`: Main extension — hold-to-talk state machine, pre-recording, tail recording, Deepgram streaming, waveform widget, double-escape editor clear
- `extensions/voice/config.ts`: Config loading, saving, migration
- `extensions/voice/onboarding.ts`: First-run setup wizard, language picker (56+ languages)
- `extensions/voice/deepgram.ts`: Deepgram URL builder, API key resolver

## Keybindings

- `SPACE` (hold ≥1.2s) — Record to editor (pre-records during warmup)
- `Ctrl+Shift+V` — Toggle recording
- `Escape` × 2 — Clear editor text

## Commands

- `/voice-setup` — Interactive setup wizard
- `/voice test` — Full diagnostics (audio tool, mic, API key validation)
- `/voice-language` — Change transcription language (56+ supported, fuzzy picker)
- `/voice-settings` — Show current voice config
- `/voice [on|off|dictate|stop|info|history]`

## Installation

```
pi install npm:@codexstar/pi-listen
```
